faq.question: Why am I seeing the error `getaddrinfo ENOTFOUND api.home-connect.com`?
faq.answer:
The `ENOTFOUND` error is a standard Node.js/system error indicating a DNS resolution failure. It means the host system running Homebridge is unable to translate the hostname `api.home-connect.com` into an IP address. This is typically caused by a network configuration issue or a temporary problem with your DNS provider.

To resolve this issue, check the following:
1. Ensure the Homebridge host has a working internet connection.
2. Test DNS resolution from a shell on the same system using `dig api.home-connect.com` or `nslookup api.home-connect.com`.
3. If your system is configured to use your router as its DNS server via DHCP, try manually setting a public DNS provider (such as Google's `8.8.8.8` or Cloudflare's `1.1.1.1`) in your operating system's network configuration.
4. Verify if any local firewall or DNS filtering (like Pi-hole or AdGuard Home) is blocking requests to `api.home-connect.com` or its underlying Amazon Web Services (AWS) endpoints.
faq.semantic_abstract: DNS resolution failures on the host system prevent the plugin from locating the Home Connect API gateway, resulting in `getaddrinfo ENOTFOUND` errors during authorisation or appliance communication.
